frameworks.name: reference repulsion
frameworks.one_line: perceptual bias where a reference skews perception of a similar feature

frameworks.name: late visual processing / decision stage
frameworks.one_line: locating the repulsion at a post-encoding comparison or decision stage

frameworks.name: early sensory encoding
frameworks.one_line: alternative account that places the bias at initial encoding

synopsis.core_question: At what stage of visual processing does an orientation reference induce repulsion of a previously seen oriented stimulus?
key_assumptions: reference and stimulus share a comparable feature dimension (orientation)
the temporal interval between stimulus and reference indexes the processing stage involved
biases at later stages should grow with stimulus-reference delay
manipulations: temporal interval between oriented stimulus and subsequent reference
presence and orientation of the reference relative to the stimulus
key_findings: a reference presented after an oriented stimulus produces repulsion in reported orientation
repulsion magnitude increases with the stimulus-to-reference temporal interval
interpretations: the reference biases representations at a late processing stage, not at initial encoding
the effect likely arises from a comparison or decision stage of visual processing
